message:
feat(abi): expand pthread condvar and mutex ABI with enhanced runtime policy support

This commit introduces comprehensive expansions to the pthread condvar and mutex
ABI implementation, alongside runtime policy enhancements. Key improvements include:

- Expanded pthread_abi.rs: Major refactoring and enhancement of condition variable
  and mutex abstractions with improved type safety and ABI correctness (176 lines)
- Runtime policy additions: New runtime_policy.rs additions enabling better control
  over pthread synchronization behavior (27 lines)
- Refined exit handling: Improvements to exit.rs for more precise exit semantics
  and integration with libc semantics (7 lines)
- Expanded condvar test coverage: Comprehensive test suite extensions in
  pthread_cond_core_test.rs covering critical condvar functionality (30 lines)
- Updated smoke tests: ld_preload_smoke.sh enhancements for improved test coverage
  in LD_PRELOAD interception scenarios (12 lines)
- Updated issues tracking: .beads/issues.jsonl updated with progress tracking

Together, these changes establish a stronger foundation for POSIX-compliant pthread
synchronization primitives and improve overall ABI stability.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -20,7 +20,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-18qq.3","title":"Fault injection: thread preemption storms","description":"Implement fault injection for thread preemption storms — workloads that maximize the probability of preemption at critical points in the TSM validation pipeline and allocator. Techniques: (1) High thread count (128+ threads) with small quanta (sched_setscheduler SCHED_RR with 1ms quantum). (2) Signal injection — send SIGUSR1 at random intervals to force preemption during critical sections. (3) CPU affinity pinning — force all threads onto 1-2 cores to maximize contention and preemption. (4) Priority inversion — create high/medium/low priority threads where medium holds a lock needed by high. (5) Thundering herd — wake all threads simultaneously (pthread_cond_broadcast) while they compete for the same allocation arena. For each pattern: verify no deadlocks (all threads complete within timeout), no data corruption (heap integrity check after storm), no lost operations (all allocations paired with frees), no livelock (progress guarantee). Measure: p99 latency under storm conditions, maximum lock hold time, number of TSM state transitions triggered by preemption.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** Concurrency stress testing from Musuvathi et al. (2008) CHESS. Priority inversion from Sha et al. (1990). Preemption testing from Sen (2008).

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- PreemptionStorm struct { thread_count: usize, quantum_us: u64, signal_interval_us: u64, affinity_cores: Vec<usize> }.
- SIGUSR1 injection: separate timer thread sending SIGUSR1 to all worker threads at random intervals.
- CPU affinity: use sched_setaffinity to pin all threads to 1-2 cores for maximum preemption.
- Priority inversion: create 3 threads with SCHED_RR priorities high/medium/low, lock ordering to trigger inversion.
- Thundering herd: pthread_cond_broadcast to wake 128 threads competing for same arena.
- Timeout: each storm has configurable timeout (default 30s). If threads do not complete, report deadlock.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
1. All 5 preemption patterns implemented and executable.
2. No deadlocks: all patterns complete within timeout (30 seconds).
3. No data corruption: heap integrity verified after each storm.
4. No lost operations: all allocations paired with frees (leak check).
5. No livelock: measurable progress in every 1-second interval.
6. p99 latency under storm < 10x baseline (bounded slowdown under extreme contention).
7. Results reproducible: same storm parameters produce completion within 2x time variance.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"**Logging Requirements:**
- Per storm: storm_type, thread_count, completion_time_ms, deadlock_detected, corruption_detected, ops_completed.
- tracing::warn!(target: preemption_storm, potential_deadlock, elapsed_ms) if progress stalls.
- Summary: preemption_storm_report.json with all storm results and per-thread operation counts.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:27:12.936668102Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:07:38.097314410Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-18qq.3","depends_on_id":"bd-18qq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:27:12.936668102Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-18qq.4","title":"E2E test: UAF detection under adversarial allocation fuzzing","description":"End-to-end test validating that use-after-free is reliably detected by the TSM under adversarial allocation patterns. Test scenario: (1) Allocate 1000 buffers of varying sizes (16B to 64KB). (2) Free 500 of them in random order. (3) Immediately begin concurrent allocation from 8 threads (to trigger arena reuse of freed slots). (4) From a separate thread, attempt to use all 500 freed pointers (read and write). (5) Verify: in strict mode, ALL 500 UAF attempts are detected and logged to evidence ledger. Zero false negatives. (6) Verify: in hardened mode, ALL 500 UAF attempts are intercepted and repaired (quarantine + safe return). (7) Verify: the generational arena's generation counter correctly identifies stale pointers even when the slot has been reallocated to a new object. (8) Stress variant: repeat with 100K buffers and 32 threads. Verify no false negatives even under extreme concurrency. This is the flagship test for FrankenLibC's core safety claim: the generational arena makes UAF detection probability P=1 (not probabilistic, but guaranteed).

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** Generational memory safety from Dhurjati & Adve (2006). UAF detection via generation counters inspired by MarkUs (Ainsworth & Jones, 2020). Zero false-negative guarantee from algebraic generation counter properties.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- UafDetectionTest: harness that allocates N buffers, frees M, triggers concurrent reuse, then attempts UAF on all M freed pointers.
- Generation check verification: for each UAF attempt, verify generation mismatch between stale pointer metadata and current slot generation.
- Evidence ledger query: assert detection entry for every UAF attempt (100% detection rate).
- Stress variant: scale to 100K buffers, 32 threads using Rayon or std::thread::scope.
- Hardened mode: verify quarantine action for each UAF (pointer placed in quarantine set, safe return value).","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
1. 500 UAF attempts detected with zero false negatives in basic test (8 threads, 1000 buffers).
2. 100K UAF attempts detected with zero false negatives in stress test (32 threads, 100K buffers).
3. Generation counter correctly identifies stale pointers even after slot reallocation.
4. Evidence ledger contains exactly one detection entry per UAF attempt (no duplicates, no misses).
5. Hardened mode quarantine + safe return for every UAF (NULL with errno=EFAULT).
6. Test completes in <60 seconds (stress variant).
7. Memory overhead of generation tracking <5% of allocated memory.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"**Logging Requirements:**
- Per UAF attempt: tracing::debug!(target: uaf_detection, ptr, stale_gen, current_gen, detected, mode, repair_action).
- Test summary: uaf_detection_report.json with total_attempts, detected, false_negatives, false_positives, detection_latency_ns.
- Evidence ledger dump: all detection cards for post-hoc verification.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FrostyBarn","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:27:23.480985901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T05:28:07.416807671Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T05:28:07.416784678Z","close_reason":"Completed: deterministic UAF adversarial E2E gate + stress variant + artifacts","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-18qq.4","depends_on_id":"bd-18qq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:27:23.480985901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-18qq.4","depends_on_id":"bd-18qq.1","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:30:19.913571502Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":351,"issue_id":"bd-18qq.4","author":"FrostyBarn","text":"Implemented deterministic adversarial UAF E2E gate in `crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/bd18qq4_uaf_adversarial_test.rs`.

What landed:
- Basic scenario: 1000 alloc / 500 free / 8-thread churn / strict+hardened assertions.
- Stress scenario (opt-in): 100000 probe attempts + 32-thread churn via env controls.
- Structured artifacts emitted to `tests/cve_arena/results/bd-18qq.4/`:
  - `trace.jsonl`
  - `uaf_adversarial_detection.v1.json`
  - `artifact_index.json`

Validation commands (all via `rch exec -- ...`):
- `cargo fmt --check` (pass)
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test bd18qq4_uaf_adversarial_test` (pass)
- `FRANKENLIBC_UAF_STRESS=1 FRANKENLIBC_UAF_STRESS_ATTEMPTS=100000 FRANKENLIBC_UAF_STRESS_THREADS=32 cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test bd18qq4_uaf_adversarial_test` (pass)
- `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` (pass with pre-existing warnings in unrelated files)
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` (fails on pre-existing unrelated issues in `frankenlibc-abi` and `frankenlibc_conformance`)
- `cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-harness --test bd18qq4_uaf_adversarial_test -- -D warnings` (pass)
","created_at":"2026-02-15T05:28:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-18qq.5","title":"E2E test: no double-free under concurrent stress","description":"End-to-end test validating that double-free is reliably detected and prevented under concurrent stress. Test scenario: (1) Allocate 10000 buffers across 16 threads (each thread owns a disjoint subset). (2) Each thread frees its buffers. (3) Inject double-free attempts: 10% of buffers are freed a second time from a different thread than the original owner. (4) Verify: in strict mode, ALL double-free attempts are detected. The TSM recognizes that the generation counter has been invalidated by the first free. Zero false negatives. (5) Verify: in hardened mode, double-free attempts are silently absorbed (no-op) rather than corrupting the free list. The evidence ledger records each detected double-free. (6) Verify: after all operations complete, heap integrity is perfect — no corrupted free list nodes, no overlapping allocations, all metadata consistent. (7) Stress variant: 100K buffers, 64 threads, 50% double-free rate. (8) Verify: no deadlocks even when double-free is detected while holding internal locks (the detection path must be lock-safe). Measurement: false positive rate (should be 0), detection latency (time between double-free attempt and detection).","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** Double-free detection from DieHard (Berger & Zorn, 2006). Generation counter approach ensures deterministic detection vs probabilistic approaches.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- DoubleFreeDection: harness that allocates, frees, then re-frees from different thread.
- Detection mechanism: generation counter invalidated on first free; second free sees already-freed generation.
- Cross-thread test: original owner frees, different thread attempts second free (tests thread-safety of detection).
- Hardened mode: second free is no-op (silently absorbed), evidence ledger records event.
- Free-list integrity: after test, walk all arena free lists verifying no corrupted nodes.","acceptance_criteria":"1. 1000 double-free attempts detected with zero false negatives (basic, 16 threads).
2. Stress: 50K double-free attempts at 50% rate with 64 threads, zero false negatives.
3. Hardened mode: double-free silently absorbed (no crash, no corruption).
4. Free-list integrity perfect after all double-free storms.
5. No deadlocks even when double-free detected during lock-held critical sections.
6. Detection latency <100ns per double-free check.
7. False positive rate exactly 0% (legitimate frees never flagged).","notes":"Implemented deterministic concurrent double-free stress coverage and mode-gated report pipeline.

Code changes:
- crates/frankenlibc-membrane/tests/allocator_membrane_invariants_sequences_test.rs
  - Added helper-driven concurrent stress harness with deterministic ownership/attacker routing.
  - Added test: concurrent_double_free_detection_basic_10k_16t_10pct
  - Added test: concurrent_double_free_detection_stress_100k_64t_50pct
  - Both tests emit structured JSON lines prefixed by DOUBLE_FREE_REPORT with detection counters and latency telemetry.
  - Validates zero false negatives, zero false positives, heap integrity post-run, and no deadlock (all threads join).
  - Added uncontended double-free latency probe field: uncontended_avg_latency_ns.
- scripts/check_double_free_stress.sh
  - New deterministic checker running strict and hardened in release mode.
  - Parses emitted JSON reports and fails on any false negatives, false positives, heap-integrity failures, or deadlock.
  - Enforces uncontended detection latency budget: uncontended_avg_latency_ns less than 100 ns.
  - Writes report artifact under target/double_free_stress/<run>/double_free_report.json.

Validation commands:
1) cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane --test allocator_membrane_invariants_sequences_test -- --nocapture
2) FRANKENLIBC_MODE=strict cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane --release --test allocator_membrane_invariants_sequences_test concurrent_double_free_detection_basic_10k_16t_10pct -- --nocapture
3) scripts/check_double_free_stress.sh

Validation highlights:
- Debug integration suite: 3 passed.
- Release gate script: PASS for strict and hardened.
- Evidence artifact: target/double_free_stress/20260213T204317Z/double_free_report.json
  - strict basic: attempts=1000 detected=1000 false_negatives=0 false_positives=0 uncontended_avg_latency_ns=20
  - strict stress: attempts=50000 detected=50000 false_negatives=0 false_positives=0 uncontended_avg_latency_ns=20
  - hardened basic: attempts=1000 detected=1000 false_negatives=0 false_positives=0 uncontended_avg_latency_ns=62
  - hardened stress: attempts=50000 detected=50000 false_negatives=0 false_positives=0 uncontended_avg_latency_ns=20","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:27:33.614463857Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T20:44:08.469746027Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T20:43:52.980306695Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-18qq.5","depends_on_id":"bd-18qq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:27:33.614463857Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-18qq.5","depends_on_id":"bd-18qq.1","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:30:20.069259391Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-18qq.6","title":"E2E test: ABI-safe strict mode under stress (real-world program execution)","description":"End-to-end test validating that strict mode is fully ABI-compatible by running real-world programs with FrankenLibC via LD_PRELOAD. Programs to test: (1) coreutils — ls, cp, cat, sort, wc with various inputs. Verify output matches glibc-linked version byte-for-byte. (2) busybox — static-linked with frankenlibc. Run test suite. (3) SQLite — run the SQLite test suite (TH3-like subset) linked against frankenlibc. Verify all tests pass. (4) Redis — start redis-server with frankenlibc, run redis-benchmark. Verify correct operation and comparable performance. (5) nginx — serve static files, verify correct HTTP responses. Stress test with wrk. For each program: (a) Verify identical output vs glibc-linked version. (b) Verify no crashes, no hangs, no memory leaks (valgrind or ASan). (c) Verify performance within 2x of glibc-linked version (strict mode overhead budget). (d) Record evidence ledger — all TSM observations during execution. (e) If any divergence, capture full reproduction case with input/output/strace. This test proves that strict mode's promise — 'drop-in replacement with zero behavior change' — holds for real programs.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** ABI compatibility testing from O'Sullivan et al. (2006). Real-world program testing from Yang et al. (2011) CSmith methodology. LD_PRELOAD interposition from Curry (1994).

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- TestProgram struct { name: &str, binary: PathBuf, args: Vec<String>, expected_output: Option<Vec<u8>>, timeout_secs: u64 }.
- Run via LD_PRELOAD=libfrankenlibc.so program args, capture stdout/stderr.
- Comparison: run same program without LD_PRELOAD (glibc), compare outputs byte-for-byte.
- Performance: measure wall time for both runs, assert frankenlibc within 2x of glibc.
- Memory check: run under valgrind --tool=memcheck with LD_PRELOAD to verify no leaks/errors.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
1. coreutils: ls, cp, cat, sort, wc produce identical output to glibc-linked versions.
2. SQLite test suite passes with frankenlibc (zero failures vs glibc baseline).
3. Redis: redis-benchmark completes with correct results and <2x latency vs glibc.
4. No crashes, no hangs for any test program (timeout enforcement at 5 minutes per program).
5. No memory leaks detected by valgrind for any test program.
6. Performance within 2x of glibc for all test programs.
7. Evidence ledger captures all TSM observations during real-world execution.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"**Logging Requirements:**
- Per program: program_name, exit_code, output_match, latency_ms, memory_peak_kb, valgrind_errors.
- Summary: abi_compat_report.json with all programs and pass/fail/perf results.
- TSM observation dump: evidence_ledger_dump.json from each program run.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DustyRiver","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:27:45.755940764Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T04:50:28.211694411Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-18qq.6","depends_on_id":"bd-18qq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:27:45.755940764Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":380,"issue_id":"bd-18qq.6","author":"DustyRiver","text":"Progress update by DustyRiver (2026-02-16 UTC):

- Completed deep architecture investigation across ABI, membrane, core, and harness crates.
- Implemented major expansion of `scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh`:
  - strict/hardened baseline-vs-preload parity checks
  - deterministic case TSV + JSON report (`abi_compat_report.json`)
  - JSONL trace emission with bead linkage
  - optional binary coverage (`busybox/sqlite3/redis-cli/nginx`) with explicit skip semantics
  - stress loop support (`STRESS_ITERS`)
- Added harness checks in `crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/e2e_suite_test.rs` for script contract markers and report/trace schema sanity.
- Validation executed:
  - `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test e2e_suite_test -- --nocapture` (pass)
  - `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` (pass after rustfmt)
  - `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` (pass)
  - `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` (pass)
  - `rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets` (fails in existing unrelated `pthread_tsd_test` assertion)

Next in-flight work:
- tighten structured trace fields to match mandatory log checklist (`api_family/symbol/decision_path/healing_action/errno/latency_ns/artifact_refs`)
- add optional memory-check hook (`valgrind` when available) and perf-ratio fields to report for acceptance alignment
- keep posting thread updates and inbox polling during execution
","created_at":"2026-02-16T04:18:16Z"},{"id":381,"issue_id":"bd-18qq.6","author":"DustyRiver","text":"Progress update by DustyRiver (2026-02-16 UTC, follow-up):

Completed additional `bd-18qq.6` implementation tightening:

- Extended `scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh` evidence contract:
  - added perf enforcement controls: `ENFORCE_PERF_MODES`, `PERF_RATIO_MAX_PPM`
  - added memory-check policy: `VALGRIND_POLICY=auto|off|required`
  - required `rch` for cargo build offload when ABI artifact is missing
  - expanded case TSV schema with perf + valgrind columns
  - expanded `abi_compat_report.json` with perf/valgrind summaries
  - expanded trace JSONL payload to include required fields:
    `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`

- Updated `crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/e2e_suite_test.rs`:
  - checks for new script contract markers (`ENFORCE_PERF_MODES`, `PERF_RATIO_MAX_PPM`, `VALGRIND_POLICY`, rch-offload requirement text)
  - validates optional new report counters (`perf_failures`, `valgrind_failures`) when present
  - validates expanded trace schema fields when present

Verification/evidence commands run:

1) Smoke script runtime/schema check:
- `TIMEOUT_SECONDS=2 STRESS_ITERS=1 VALGRIND_POLICY=off ENFORCE_PARITY_MODES=strict ENFORCE_PERF_MODES=strict bash scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh`
- result: expected strict/hardened failures under current ABI runtime; artifacts produced at:
  - `target/ld_preload_smoke/20260216T043212Z/abi_compat_report.json`
  - `target/ld_preload_smoke/20260216T043212Z/trace.jsonl`

2) Required quality gates (rch-offloaded):
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test e2e_suite_test -- --nocapture` ✅

3) Full workspace test sweep:
- `rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets`
- progressed through substantial test/bench output and many suites passed, but run became long/stalled in pthread thread lifecycle area with no further output; process was terminated from this session to avoid indefinite hang. Not marked as a clean full pass in this iteration.

Current bead state recommendation:
- Keep `bd-18qq.6` as `in_progress` (strict ABI parity still failing for multiple real programs; coverage/evidence tooling now significantly improved).
","created_at":"2026-02-16T04:42:11Z"},{"id":382,"issue_id":"bd-18qq.6","author":"DustyRiver","text":"Progress update by DustyRiver (2026-02-16 UTC, signal-path stabilization):

New concrete fix landed on `main`:
- commit: `9ec4316`
- change: `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/signal_abi.rs` now passes kernel-sized sigset length (`sizeof(c_ulong)`) to `rt_sigaction` syscall instead of libc userspace `sigset_t` size.
- added integration regression tests: `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/signal_abi_test.rs`
  - `sigaction_query_sigpipe_succeeds`
  - `signal_sigpipe_install_and_restore_succeeds`

Validation (rch-offloaded):
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test signal_abi_test -- --nocapture` ✅ (2/2)
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅

E2E follow-up (remote fresh release artifact):
- `rch exec -- cargo build -p frankenlibc-abi --release` ✅
- `rch exec -- env TIMEOUT_SECONDS=2 STRESS_ITERS=0 VALGRIND_POLICY=off ENFORCE_PARITY_MODES=strict ENFORCE_PERF_MODES=strict bash scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh` ❌ (expected broad failures remain)

Key improvement:
- prior fatal assertion `signal(libc::SIGPIPE, handler) != libc::SIG_ERR` no longer appears in strict/hardened smoke output.
- failure surface moved deeper to runtime kernel panics / aborts / segfaults (example strict `coreutils_echo` stderr now shows runtime kernel panic fallback and abort).

Latest artifact set:
- `target/ld_preload_smoke/20260216T044924Z/abi_compat_report.json`
- `target/ld_preload_smoke/20260216T044924Z/trace.jsonl`
- `target/ld_preload_smoke/20260216T044924Z/strict/coreutils_echo/stderr.txt`

Current blocker focus for bd-18qq.6:
- investigate runtime-policy/kernel panic path now exposed after signal ABI fix; stabilize strict-mode non-trivial binary startup under LD_PRELOAD.
","created_at":"2026-02-16T04:50:28Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-18qq.6","title":"E2E test: ABI-safe strict mode under stress (real-world program execution)","description":"End-to-end test validating that strict mode is fully ABI-compatible by running real-world programs with FrankenLibC via LD_PRELOAD. Programs to test: (1) coreutils — ls, cp, cat, sort, wc with various inputs. Verify output matches glibc-linked version byte-for-byte. (2) busybox — static-linked with frankenlibc. Run test suite. (3) SQLite — run the SQLite test suite (TH3-like subset) linked against frankenlibc. Verify all tests pass. (4) Redis — start redis-server with frankenlibc, run redis-benchmark. Verify correct operation and comparable performance. (5) nginx — serve static files, verify correct HTTP responses. Stress test with wrk. For each program: (a) Verify identical output vs glibc-linked version. (b) Verify no crashes, no hangs, no memory leaks (valgrind or ASan). (c) Verify performance within 2x of glibc-linked version (strict mode overhead budget). (d) Record evidence ledger — all TSM observations during execution. (e) If any divergence, capture full reproduction case with input/output/strace. This test proves that strict mode's promise — 'drop-in replacement with zero behavior change' — holds for real programs.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** ABI compatibility testing from O'Sullivan et al. (2006). Real-world program testing from Yang et al. (2011) CSmith methodology. LD_PRELOAD interposition from Curry (1994).

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- TestProgram struct { name: &str, binary: PathBuf, args: Vec<String>, expected_output: Option<Vec<u8>>, timeout_secs: u64 }.
- Run via LD_PRELOAD=libfrankenlibc.so program args, capture stdout/stderr.
- Comparison: run same program without LD_PRELOAD (glibc), compare outputs byte-for-byte.
- Performance: measure wall time for both runs, assert frankenlibc within 2x of glibc.
- Memory check: run under valgrind --tool=memcheck with LD_PRELOAD to verify no leaks/errors.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
1. coreutils: ls, cp, cat, sort, wc produce identical output to glibc-linked versions.
2. SQLite test suite passes with frankenlibc (zero failures vs glibc baseline).
3. Redis: redis-benchmark completes with correct results and <2x latency vs glibc.
4. No crashes, no hangs for any test program (timeout enforcement at 5 minutes per program).
5. No memory leaks detected by valgrind for any test program.
6. Performance within 2x of glibc for all test programs.
7. Evidence ledger captures all TSM observations during real-world execution.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"**Logging Requirements:**
- Per program: program_name, exit_code, output_match, latency_ms, memory_peak_kb, valgrind_errors.
- Summary: abi_compat_report.json with all programs and pass/fail/perf results.
- TSM observation dump: evidence_ledger_dump.json from each program run.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DustyRiver","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:27:45.755940764Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T05:57:57.252806883Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-18qq.6","depends_on_id":"bd-18qq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:27:45.755940764Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":380,"issue_id":"bd-18qq.6","author":"DustyRiver","text":"Progress update by DustyRiver (2026-02-16 UTC):

- Completed deep architecture investigation across ABI, membrane, core, and harness crates.

  - strict/hardened baseline-vs-preload parity checks
  - deterministic case TSV + JSON report (`abi_compat_report.json`)

  - optional binary coverage (`busybox/sqlite3/redis-cli/nginx`) with explicit skip semantics
  - stress loop support (`STRESS_ITERS`)
- Added harness checks in `crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/e2e_suite_test.rs` for script contract markers and report/trace schema sanity.
- Validation executed:
  - `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test e2e_suite_test -- --nocapture` (pass)
  - `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` (pass after rustfmt)
  - `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` (pass)

Next in-flight work:

- add optional memory-check hook (`valgrind` when available) and perf-ratio fields to report for acceptance alignment
- keep posting thread updates and inbox polling during execution
","created_at":"2026-02-16T04:18:16Z"},{"id":381,"issue_id":"bd-18qq.6","author":"DustyRiver","text":"Progress update by DustyRiver (2026-02-16 UTC, follow-up):

Completed additional `bd-18qq.6` implementation tightening:

- Extended `scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh` evidence contract:
  - added perf enforcement controls: `ENFORCE_PERF_MODES`, `PERF_RATIO_MAX_PPM`
  - added memory-check policy: `VALGRIND_POLICY=auto|off|required`
  - required `rch` for cargo build offload when ABI artifact is missing
  - expanded case TSV schema with perf + valgrind columns
  - expanded `abi_compat_report.json` with perf/valgrind summaries
  - expanded trace JSONL payload to include required fields:
    `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`

- Updated `crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/e2e_suite_test.rs`:
  - checks for new script contract markers (`ENFORCE_PERF_MODES`, `PERF_RATIO_MAX_PPM`, `VALGRIND_POLICY`, rch-offload requirement text)
  - validates optional new report counters (`perf_failures`, `valgrind_failures`) when present
  - validates expanded trace schema fields when present

Verification/evidence commands run:

1) Smoke script runtime/schema check:
- `TIMEOUT_SECONDS=2 STRESS_ITERS=1 VALGRIND_POLICY=off ENFORCE_PARITY_MODES=strict ENFORCE_PERF_MODES=strict bash scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh`
- result: expected strict/hardened failures under current ABI runtime; artifacts produced at:
  - `target/ld_preload_smoke/20260216T043212Z/abi_compat_report.json`
  - `target/ld_preload_smoke/20260216T043212Z/trace.jsonl`

2) Required quality gates (rch-offloaded):
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test e2e_suite_test -- --nocapture` ✅

3) Full workspace test sweep:
- `rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets`
- progressed through substantial test/bench output and many suites passed, but run became long/stalled in pthread thread lifecycle area with no further output; process was terminated from this session to avoid indefinite hang. Not marked as a clean full pass in this iteration.

Current bead state recommendation:
- Keep `bd-18qq.6` as `in_progress` (strict ABI parity still failing for multiple real programs; coverage/evidence tooling now significantly improved).
","created_at":"2026-02-16T04:42:11Z"},{"id":382,"issue_id":"bd-18qq.6","author":"DustyRiver","text":"Progress update by DustyRiver (2026-02-16 UTC, signal-path stabilization):

New concrete fix landed on `main`:
- commit: `9ec4316`
- change: `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/signal_abi.rs` now passes kernel-sized sigset length (`sizeof(c_ulong)`) to `rt_sigaction` syscall instead of libc userspace `sigset_t` size.
- added integration regression tests: `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/signal_abi_test.rs`
  - `sigaction_query_sigpipe_succeeds`
  - `signal_sigpipe_install_and_restore_succeeds`

Validation (rch-offloaded):
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test signal_abi_test -- --nocapture` ✅ (2/2)
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅

E2E follow-up (remote fresh release artifact):
- `rch exec -- cargo build -p frankenlibc-abi --release` ✅
- `rch exec -- env TIMEOUT_SECONDS=2 STRESS_ITERS=0 VALGRIND_POLICY=off ENFORCE_PARITY_MODES=strict ENFORCE_PERF_MODES=strict bash scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh` ❌ (expected broad failures remain)

Key improvement:
- prior fatal assertion `signal(libc::SIGPIPE, handler) != libc::SIG_ERR` no longer appears in strict/hardened smoke output.
- failure surface moved deeper to runtime kernel panics / aborts / segfaults (example strict `coreutils_echo` stderr now shows runtime kernel panic fallback and abort).

Latest artifact set:
- `target/ld_preload_smoke/20260216T044924Z/abi_compat_report.json`
- `target/ld_preload_smoke/20260216T044924Z/trace.jsonl`
- `target/ld_preload_smoke/20260216T044924Z/strict/coreutils_echo/stderr.txt`

Current blocker focus for bd-18qq.6:
- investigate runtime-policy/kernel panic path now exposed after signal ABI fix; stabilize strict-mode non-trivial binary startup under LD_PRELOAD.
","created_at":"2026-02-16T04:50:28Z"},{"id":383,"issue_id":"bd-18qq.6","author":"DustyRiver","text":"Progress update by DustyRiver (2026-02-16 UTC, signal ABI contract refinement):

Additional fix committed on `main`:
- commit: `e557436`
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/signal_abi.rs`
  - changed `signal` ABI signature to use `libc::sighandler_t` (correct C ABI contract for SIG_DFL/SIG_IGN/SIG_ERR sentinels)
  - retained kernel-sized sigset fix for `rt_sigaction`
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/signal_abi_test.rs`
  - expanded to 3 integration tests, including SIG_IGN roundtrip

Validation (rch-offloaded):
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test signal_abi_test -- --nocapture` ✅ (3/3)
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅

Behavior probe on fresh remote release artifact:
- `rch exec -- cargo build -p frankenlibc-abi --release` ✅
- `rch exec -- env FRANKENLIBC_MODE=strict LD_PRELOAD=target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so /bin/echo panic_probe_after_rebuild`
  - output prints normally, prior SIGPIPE assertion no longer appears
  - process still aborts with runtime-kernel panic fallback after execution

Interpretation:
- SIGPIPE assertion class is resolved.
- Remaining blocker is deeper runtime-policy/kernel panic path during process lifecycle, now clearly isolated for next debug iteration.
","created_at":"2026-02-16T04:53:32Z"},{"id":384,"issue_id":"bd-18qq.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update by DustyRiver (2026-02-16 UTC, exit-recursion root cause + mitigation):

Root-cause isolated and fixed for the strict-mode abort seen after successful `/bin/echo` output:

- Observability improvement in `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/runtime_policy.rs` panic hook:
  - added first-occurrence panic payload logging (`frankenlibc: panic payload: ...`) via raw `sys_write`
  - this exposed the concrete payload: `std::process::exit called re-entrantly`

- Functional fix in `crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdlib/exit.rs`:
  - replaced `std::process::exit(status)` with raw `syscall::sys_exit_group(status)`
  - avoids recursive re-entry through our interposed `exit` ABI path under `LD_PRELOAD`

Evidence/probes:

1) Before fix (with payload logging):
- `env FRANKENLIBC_MODE=strict LD_PRELOAD=.../libfrankenlibc_abi.so /bin/echo ...`
- panic payload showed: `std::process::exit called re-entrantly`

2) After fix:
- `env FRANKENLIBC_MODE=strict LD_PRELOAD=/data/projects/frankenlibc/target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so /bin/echo panic_probe_after_exit_fix_local`
- exits cleanly (no abort/panic)

3) Smoke rerun artifact (strict/hardened):
- `target/ld_preload_smoke/20260216T050721Z/abi_compat_report.json`
- prior re-entrant exit panic class removed from `coreutils_echo`
- remaining failures now centered on:
  - segfault/timeout in `coreutils_ls_tmp`, `sort`, `wc`, `redis-cli`
  - Python GIL init failure (`PyCOND_INIT(gil->cond) failed`) in both modes

Quality gates / validation (rch-offloaded for cargo-heavy commands):
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi runtime_policy -- --nocapture` ✅ (runtime_policy tests passed)
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test signal_abi_test -- --nocapture` ✅
- attempted `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-core -- --nocapture`; run progressed deeply then hung in pthread thread lifecycle tests and was terminated from this session (not a clean full pass in this iteration)

Next focus for `bd-18qq.6`:
- triage segfault path in `coreutils_ls_tmp` (strict/hardened) and Python condvar init failure under preload.
","created_at":"2026-02-16T05:15:18Z"},{"id":385,"issue_id":"bd-18qq.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update by DustyRiver (2026-02-16 UTC, pthread condattr compatibility hardening):

Follow-up on the Python strict-mode blocker:

Implemented in `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs`:
- `pthread_cond_init` now accepts non-null cond attributes instead of hard-failing.
- It attempts `pthread_condattr_getclock(attr, &clock_id)` and uses that clock when available.
- If `getclock` fails, it now falls back to `PTHREAD_COND_CLOCK_REALTIME` instead of returning `EINVAL`.

Added/updated regression tests:
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/pthread_cond_core_test.rs`
  - replaced attr-reject test with attr-acceptance coverage
  - added monotonic-clock attribute acceptance test

Validation (cargo-heavy checks via rch where available):
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-abi --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_cond_core_test -- --nocapture` ✅
  - note: one run reported daemon unavailable and executed locally via rch fail-open path

Runtime probes:
- `/tmp/cond_probe` under preload now reports `cond_init=0` (was `22` before this patch)
- `python3 -c 'print(1)'` under preload is no longer immediate fatal `PyCOND_INIT` abort; it now times out/hangs (remaining blocker moved deeper into pthread/runtime behavior)

Current smoke artifact after patch:
- `target/ld_preload_smoke/20260216T053620Z/abi_compat_report.json`
- `python3_print` now fails by timeout (not fatal init error), while existing segfault/timeout classes remain for ls/sort/wc/redis.

Status:
- `bd-18qq.6` remains in progress; strict-mode real-program parity still not met, but fatal exit recursion and immediate python cond-init fatal class are both mitigated.
","created_at":"2026-02-16T05:37:07Z"},{"id":386,"issue_id":"bd-18qq.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update by DustyRiver (2026-02-16 UTC, strict pthread path + smoke parity normalization):

Key behavior changes validated:

1) Strict preload runtime probes now pass for previously failing binaries:
- `python3 -c 'print(1)'` under strict preload now exits `0`.
- strict probes for `/bin/ls`, `/bin/sort`, `/usr/bin/wc`, `redis-cli --version` all exit `0`.

2) `pthread` ABI compatibility improvements:
- `pthread_cond_init` accepts non-null attrs and honors attr clock when readable, fallback realtime on probe failure.
- `pthread_cond_wait` / `pthread_cond_timedwait` no longer require managed-mutex magic and now enforce caller-held lock via non-zero lock-word heuristic.

3) Smoke harness parity correction:
- `scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh` updated so `coreutils_env` strict stdout parity ignores harness-injected env vars only:
  - `FRANKENLIBC_MODE`
  - `LD_PRELOAD`
- This avoids false strict parity failure caused solely by harness injection, not ABI behavior drift.

Validation executed:
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-abi --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_cond_core_test -- --nocapture` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test e2e_suite_test -- --nocapture` ✅ (13/13)
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` ✅

Latest smoke evidence (reduced loop for fast regression signal):
- command: `TIMEOUT_SECONDS=5 STRESS_ITERS=1 ENFORCE_PERF_MODES=none VALGRIND_POLICY=off scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh`
- result: `passes=30 fails=0 skips=2` (PASS)
- artifact: `target/ld_preload_smoke/20260216T054438Z/abi_compat_report.json`
- trace: `target/ld_preload_smoke/20260216T054438Z/trace.jsonl`

Status:
- `bd-18qq.6` remains `in_progress` pending full acceptance sweep scope (expanded stress/perf and additional real-program matrices), but strict-mode crash/hang blockers from this iteration are materially reduced.
","created_at":"2026-02-16T05:45:55Z"},{"id":387,"issue_id":"bd-18qq.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update by DustyRiver (2026-02-16 UTC, release-mode regression triage):

I continued `bd-18qq.6` in optimized release-mode validation only (debug LD_PRELOAD was confirmed non-representative due `#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), no_mangle)]` gating on many interposed symbols).

New implementation work:

1) `pthread_cond_*` host delegation path (ABI alignment with existing mutex delegation):
- Added host symbol delegates for:
  - `pthread_cond_init`
  - `pthread_cond_destroy`
  - `pthread_cond_wait`
  - `pthread_cond_signal`
  - `pthread_cond_broadcast`
  - `pthread_cond_timedwait`
- Delegation is enabled by default and falls back to native path when native mutex test mode is forced.

2) Host symbol version resolution fix:
- Updated `resolve_host_symbol` order to prefer `GLIBC_2.3.2` before `GLIBC_2.2.5`.
- Rationale: `pthread_cond_*` exports both versions; binding `GLIBC_2.2.5` caused attr incompatibility (`cond_init=EINVAL`) under preload.

Validation:
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-abi --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_cond_core_test -- --nocapture` ✅

Release-mode probe outcomes after syncing fresh remote artifact into local `target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so`:
- `/tmp/cond_probe` now returns `cond_init=0` again ✅ (fixed from prior `22` regression).
- Remaining blockers persist:
  - `python3 -c 'print(1)'` times out (rc=124)
  - `/bin/ls -la /tmp` segfaults (rc=139)
  - `/bin/sort` and `/usr/bin/wc` time out
  - `redis-cli --version` times out

Latest smoke artifact:
- command: `TIMEOUT_SECONDS=5 STRESS_ITERS=1 ENFORCE_PERF_MODES=strict VALGRIND_POLICY=off scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh`
- result: `passes=16 fails=14 skips=2`
- report: `target/ld_preload_smoke/20260216T055422Z/abi_compat_report.json`
- trace: `target/ld_preload_smoke/20260216T055422Z/trace.jsonl`

Current status:
- `bd-18qq.6` remains `in_progress`.
- This iteration fixed a release-only host condattr compatibility regression but did not yet eliminate the remaining strict/hardened runtime hang/segfault class.
","created_at":"2026-02-16T05:57:57Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-18qq.7","title":"Conformance: POSIX conformance tests for all 250 symbols","description":"Implement POSIX conformance tests for all 250 implemented symbols, verifying behavior matches the POSIX.1-2024 specification. For each symbol: (1) Extract test vectors from the POSIX specification — required behavior for valid inputs, required error codes for invalid inputs, required side effects. (2) Implement deterministic test cases covering: (a) Normal operation with typical inputs. (b) Boundary conditions specified by POSIX (e.g., SIZE_MAX for size_t params, LONG_MAX for long params). (c) Error conditions — each documented errno value must be tested. (d) Thread-safety — functions marked MT-Safe must be tested under concurrent access. (e) Async-signal-safety — functions marked AS-Safe must be tested from signal handlers. (f) Cancellation points — functions that are cancellation points must be tested with pthread_cancel. (3) Map each test to its POSIX specification section for traceability. (4) Generate conformance report: symbol x requirement -> pass/fail/not_applicable. (5) CI gate: POSIX conformance must not regress. New implementations must include conformance tests before merge. Complements the existing POSIX test suite epic (bd-2tq) by providing comprehensive per-symbol coverage rather than per-family coverage.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** POSIX.1-2024 specification. Test methodology from Open POSIX Test Suite (IEEE 2003). Conformance matrix design from NIST validation methodology.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- PosixConformanceTest struct { symbol: &str, posix_section: &str, test_vector: TestVector, expected: ExpectedBehavior }.
- Test vectors: extracted from POSIX specification text, encoded as structured test data.
- Thread-safety (MT-Safe): tested via concurrent invocation from N threads with shared state.
- Async-signal-safety (AS-Safe): tested by calling function from within SIGUSR1 handler.
- Cancellation points: tested via pthread_cancel during function execution.
- Traceability: each test linked to POSIX specification section and paragraph number.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
1. All 250 symbols have at least 3 conformance tests each (normal, boundary, error).
2. All documented errno values tested for each symbol that can fail.
3. MT-Safe functions tested under 16-thread concurrent access with zero data races.
4. AS-Safe functions tested from signal handler context with zero deadlocks.
5. Cancellation points tested with pthread_cancel (at least 10 cancellable functions).
6. Traceability matrix: 100% of tests linked to POSIX specification section.
7. Conformance report: symbol x requirement -> pass/fail/not_applicable as JSON artifact.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"**Logging Requirements:**
- Per test: symbol, posix_section, test_type, input, expected, actual, result.
- Summary: posix_conformance_report.json with aggregate pass/fail/skip counts per family.
- CI gate: conformance regression report comparing against previous release baseline.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:27:55.958235220Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:07:27.065953992Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-18qq.7","depends_on_id":"bd-18qq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:27:55.958235220Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-18qq.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2tq","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:30:12.100625362Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-18rq","title":"bd-yos subtask: Threading hotspot optimization loop with behavior proofs","description":"Background:
- Thread operations are hot in many workloads; we need bounded overhead with proof-backed optimization.

Goal:
- Run profile-driven optimization pass over pthread_create/join critical sections.

Deliverables:
1) Baseline/profile metrics for start/join paths.
2) Opportunity matrix and selected optimization candidate(s).
3) Isomorphism proof record and regression checks.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Either measurable improvement achieved or documented no-change rationale.
- No behavior regression in strict/hardened functional suites.
